items.title: Billionaire Investor Ron Baron Believes SpaceX Will Be Worth $30 Trillion
items.summary: Ron Baron, CEO of Baron Capital, believes SpaceX could reach a valuation of $10-30 trillion within 10-20 years, driven primarily by Starlink's growth to 300 million users and $1 trillion in revenue. However, the article notes SpaceX's current $1.75 trillion valuation already prices in perfection, with the company facing challenges in justifying its 100x sales multiple amid slowing revenue growth and widening losses.

items.title: WINTON GROUP Ltd Acquires New Stake in Tesla, Inc. $TSLA - MarketBeat
items.summary: WINTON GROUP Ltd Acquires New Stake in Tesla, Inc. $TSLA    MarketBeat

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Is Weeks From Going Public—Polymarket Trader Are Betting It Will Be Worth $2 Trillion
items.summary: SpaceX is weeks away from its IPO with Polymarket bettors predicting a 75% chance the company's market cap will exceed $2 trillion post-listing. The company is targeting 555.6 million shares at $135 per share. Speculation continues about a potential 2027 merger with Tesla, which could impact Elon Musk's compensation structure.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Targets $75 Billion IPO Raise, Plans To Sell Over 555 Million Shares At $135 Apiece: Report
items.summary: SpaceX is targeting a $75 billion IPO by selling 555.6 million shares at $135 per share in the coming weeks. The company outlined a $28.5 trillion market opportunity, primarily tied to AI pursuits. A potential Tesla-SpaceX merger could trigger Musk's $1 trillion CEO pay package automatically, though Tesla stock declined following SEC filing updates signaling potential dilution concerns.

items.title: Is Elon Musk Selling 'Hopium' With the $1.8 Trillion SpaceX IPO?
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for an IPO with an $1.8 trillion valuation, which would make it the eighth-largest U.S. company. While the company has impressive revenue growth ($18.7B in 2025, up 33% YoY) and Starlink profitability, critics argue the valuation is unjustified. SpaceX's 96x price-to-sales ratio far exceeds Tesla's 15.7x, and the company's $28.5 trillion total addressable market estimate is largely based on speculative AI data center plans rather than current business fundamentals.

items.title: Why a Tesla and SpaceX Merger Would Not Be Great News for Tesla Shareholders
items.summary: While a potential Tesla-SpaceX merger could offer benefits like securing chip supply chains through their Terafab joint venture, the article argues it would be detrimental to Tesla shareholders. A merger would dilute Tesla's value precisely as the company is poised to scale major initiatives like robotaxis and Optimus robots in 2026-2027. The trend in industrial sectors favors focused companies over conglomerates, and Tesla can fund its own growth without relying on SpaceX.

items.title: Is Tesla Stock Your Ticket to Becoming a Millionaire?
items.summary: While Tesla's stock has delivered exceptional historical returns (22,250% over 15 years), the article argues it is not a reliable millionaire-maker opportunity today. The company faces slowing growth, rising inventory, and intense EV competition. CEO Elon Musk is pivoting toward autonomous driving and humanoid robots, requiring $25+ billion in capital expenditures, but success is uncertain. Tesla's extremely high P/E ratio of 402 reflects lofty market expectations that may be difficult to meet.

items.title: 2 Reasons It's Time for Savvy Investors to Buy Ford Now
items.summary: Ford Motor Company is transforming its business model through two high-margin ventures: Ford Energy, a new energy storage system for data centers and utilities that could generate $500-600 million in annual EBIT by 2027, and Ford Pro, its commercial vehicle division that posted $6.8 billion EBIT with a 10.3% margin in 2025, including growing software subscription services. These developments position Ford for higher valuations after a decade of stagnant stock performance.

items.title: 2 Monster EV Stocks Chasing a $10 Trillion AI Opportunity
items.summary: The article highlights how EV companies are positioned to capitalize on a $10 trillion robotaxi market opportunity powered by AI advances. Tesla and Rivian are identified as the two best-positioned EV stocks to benefit from the emerging autonomous taxi market, which is expected to scale globally by 2030. Tesla has established self-driving capabilities and a $20 billion capex plan for AI investments, while Rivian is positioned as a supplier to robotaxi providers, including a $1.25 billion deal with Uber for 50,000 vehicles.

items.title: Why Tesla Robotaxi Dreams Can’t Rescue Today’s Weak Fundamentals
items.summary: Tesla's valuation of $1.3 trillion is heavily dependent on unproven robotaxi and AI technologies, with roughly $1.25 trillion assigned to businesses that don't yet exist. The company faces near-term headwinds including stagnant vehicle deliveries, compressed margins (17-18%), declining regulatory credit revenue, and intensifying competition from legacy automakers and Chinese EV makers. Without successful autonomy deployment, Tesla would be valued as a mature automaker worth $50-100 billion, representing a 90%+ downside from current levels.

items.title: Is China's EV Boom Losing Steam? Tesla Rival Nio's CEO Sounds The Alarm
items.summary: Nio CEO William Li warns that China's automotive sector has passed its 'golden era,' with domestic car sales expected to stagnate in 2026 despite the company's 112% revenue growth. Tesla has dropped out of China's top ten EV companies, while BYD continues declining for the eighth consecutive month. Nio plans to increase spending on autonomous driving development fivefold but remains focused on the Chinese market with minimal overseas presence.

items.title: SpaceX Could Be Worth More Than Tesla the Moment It Goes Public. Here's Why.
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a historic IPO targeting a $1.75-2 trillion valuation on Nasdaq in June, which would make it larger than Tesla on day one. While SpaceX posted a net loss of $4.9 billion in 2025, its Starlink satellite internet business is booming with $11.4 billion in revenue (up 50% YoY), 10.3 million subscribers, and $7.2 billion in adjusted EBITDA. In contrast, Tesla faces declining vehicle sales and is investing heavily in speculative AI and robotaxi projects, trading at a 400x P/E ratio despite slowing growth.

items.title: EV Sales Skyrocket: Tesla Stock Surges On 67% European Boom, Blockbuster SpaceX IPO Rumors
items.summary: Tesla stock gained 1.11% on Wednesday, driven by strong European EV sales growth (67.2% year-over-year in April) and speculation about a potential future merger with SpaceX. The SpaceX IPO is expected on Nasdaq under ticker SPCX following its merger with xAI, valued at $1.25 trillion. Tesla continues expanding beyond traditional auto manufacturing into AI, robotics, and energy sectors, with the next earnings catalyst expected on July 22.

items.title: Tesla’s European Recovery Is Real, But BYD Defines the Limit
items.summary: Tesla shows genuine recovery in European EV registrations with three consecutive months of year-on-year growth, including a 46.5% rebound in April 2026 to 10,654 units. However, BYD's dominance—registering 27,008 units in the same month with a broader product portfolio—defines structural competitive limits. Tesla's recovery is driven by fuel-price-driven EV demand, base effects, and regulatory approvals (FSD in Netherlands), but BYD's aggressive European expansion and lower-priced offerings pose sustained competitive pressure despite BYD's domestic margin compression.

items.title: Ross Gerber Backs SpaceX IPO Following Successful Starship V3 Test Launch: 'Perfect Timing For...'
items.summary: Investor Ross Gerber has endorsed SpaceX's upcoming IPO following a successful Starship rocket test launch, calling it 'perfect timing.' SpaceX plans to allocate up to 30% of shares to retail investors through platforms like Robinhood and SoFi, with Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley serving as lead underwriters. However, some experts warn the IPO could negatively impact Tesla investors by diverting CEO Elon Musk's attention.

items.title: Here Are 7 Important Things Investors Learned from SpaceX's S-1 Filing
items.summary: SpaceX filed its S-1 prospectus ahead of its IPO, seeking a $2 trillion valuation and $75 billion in funding. While its Starlink connectivity business is highly profitable with 10.3 million subscribers and 50%+ revenue growth, it's subsidizing massive losses in the unprofitable space and AI segments. The company's valuation of 107x trailing sales appears expensive given slowing growth and aggressive AI spending plans that will likely remain a money pit.

items.title: How 1 Top Global EV Maker Adjusted to the Iran Conflict and Is Thriving
items.summary: BYD demonstrated remarkable business resilience by quickly pivoting exports away from the Middle East to Europe after the Iran conflict disrupted shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite a 60% drop in March Middle East deliveries, the company offset losses by increasing European sales and raised its 2026 export guidance from 1.3 million to 1.5 million vehicles, showcasing the flexibility advantages of Chinese automakers' export-focused factories and low-cost production.

items.title: Tesla Investors Beware? SpaceX IPO Could Pull Cash, Attention And Musk Away From EV Giant
items.summary: SpaceX's upcoming IPO could negatively impact Tesla as it may divert investor attention, capital, and Elon Musk's focus away from the EV company. Experts warn that the IPO could pull retail investors from Tesla into SpaceX, though some suggest a merger between the two companies could be beneficial. The effects on Tesla shares may not be immediately felt but could emerge within three months of SpaceX's listing.

items.title: SpaceX Finally Made It's S-1 Public. 3 Things Smart Investors Need to Know About the IPO
items.summary: SpaceX has filed its S-1 prospectus for what could be the largest IPO in history, with a projected valuation of $1.75-$2 trillion. The company reported $18.67 billion in sales but a $4.9 billion net loss in 2025, with Q1 2026 showing a $4.27 billion loss on $4.69 billion in sales. While SpaceX has strong growth potential in rocket services and satellite internet (Starlink), investors should note that CEO Elon Musk controls 85.1% of voting power and the company's ambitious $28.5 trillion total addressable market estimates require significant execution.

items.title: Tesla Model S Curtain Call: Here's How Much The Stock Gained Since Launch
items.summary: Tesla has ended production of the Model S and Model X after 14 years and over 750,000 units produced. Since the Model S launch on June 22, 2012, Tesla stock has gained 18,476.4%, turning a $1,000 investment into $185,762.59. Tesla is now focusing on robotaxis and the Optimus Bot, which CEO Elon Musk believes could drive the company to a $20-30 trillion valuation.

items.title: Is a SpaceX-Tesla Merger Elon Musk's Endgame? Here Are 4 Factors Investors Need to Consider.
items.summary: Following SpaceX's planned $75 billion IPO that would value the company at ~$2 trillion, rumors suggest Elon Musk may merge SpaceX with Tesla. While such a merger could create synergies across rockets, AI, robotics, and EVs, it would face significant execution risks, regulatory hurdles including antitrust scrutiny, and operational complexity at a critical time when Tesla faces Chinese competition and SpaceX burns $1 billion monthly on xAI scaling. SpaceX's dual-class share structure gives Musk 50% voting control post-IPO, making the merger feasible if pursued.

items.title: Here's When Tesla's Robotaxi Rollout Will Really Ramp Up
items.summary: Tesla is aggressively expanding its robotaxi fleet, currently operating 39 unsupervised robotaxis across Austin, Dallas, and Houston. CEO Elon Musk clarified that the company will wait for Full Self-Driving v15 software release (expected early 2027) before launching a large-scale rollout, as major architectural improvements are needed to enhance safety. The company is preparing for expansion into Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas in the meantime.

items.title: Elon Musk Accused Of Building 'Most Management-Favorable' IPO Structure At SpaceX: Report
items.summary: Pension officials from New York and California have criticized SpaceX's proposed IPO governance structure as 'extremely' management-favorable, citing concerns over Musk's concentrated voting power through dual-class shares and his control over CEO removal. The officials urged SpaceX to adopt a one-vote, one-share structure and separate the Chair and CEO roles. SpaceX is targeting a $1.75 trillion valuation for its IPO.
